title: Volume path fixes
body:
Fixes #2395 

Several fixes for our handling of volume path. Add it to libpod.conf so it can be manually set, change its location when storage is moved, and validate it against the DB.

That might be the DB checks I added overriding the config file... I really
ought to add a debug line about that. Will do further testing today, and
see about adding a test, we can probably inspect the volume and see what
directory it ended up in.

On Mon, Feb 25, 2019, 05:22 Valentin Rothberg <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> *@vrothberg* commented on this pull request.
>
> It does not seem to work on my machine or am I missing something?
>
> [valentin@nebuchadnezzar libpod]$ head -n1 /usr/share/containers/libpod.conf
> volume_path = "/home/valentin/libpod-volumes"
> [valentin@nebuchadnezzar libpod]$ sudo ./bin/podman volume create testtest
> [valentin@nebuchadnezzar libpod]$ sudo ./bin/podman volume inspect test
> [
>     {
>         "name": "test",
>         "labels": {},
>         "mountPoint": "/var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/test/_data",
>         "driver": "local",
>         "options": {},
>         "scope": "local"
>     }
>
> Can we add some tests to avoid regressions on it?

In the context of the `podman info --debug` PR. Maybe we can add a `VolumePath` field to the `store` table?
Added to inspect. Did some tests, and we are definitely respecting `--volume-path` from the CLI. Will test `volume_path` in libpod.conf next.
Confirmed that we are not respecting `volume_path` in libpod.conf - looking into it

@vrothberg @rhatdan @baude I don't think we can get a fix for the issue @vrothberg pointed out into 1.1 - too many changes. I think we should merge this as-is to at least ensure volume path is a subdirectory of c/storage
@mheon Get it to pass tests and we can merge.
Flakes on both failed tests, restarted

Dear god it's green
@rhatdan @baude Someone want to slap a final LGTM on here
Would like a head nod from @vrothberg
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #2362) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
